text: Dirtus presents all twelve slides. It takes forty minutes. Nobody leaves.

The summer convergence plan involves: a rented canoe (Dirtus has identified a lake near Scatha's estimated underground meridian), a waterproof version of the Scathagram drawn on a floating platform, and a new hymn written collaboratively by all five members. Week by week. Through the summer.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I want to note that this plan is insane.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I also want to note that I looked up the lake. It's actually nice. Good fishing.

speaker: Mawla
text: I've already started writing something for the summer hymn.
The sun is going down through the clubroom window. The worm poster glows in the orange light. The clay figure on the windowsill, cleaned now, looks wise.

Dirtus is already talking about slide two of the summer plan. Grubwin is writing notes on his clipboard, real notes, helpful notes. Vermia is sketching a new hymn structure in the margin of her Rhyme Journal. Mawla is reading something she wrote to herself, quietly, making small changes.
And you are here. In this room. Among these people.
There is something beneath the surface of things. Something that turns slowly, that doesn't ask permission, that moves through darkness like it belongs.
You don't know if it's Scatha. You don't know if it's anything with a name.
